project.name: Recipe Radar
project.goal: Turn a photo of a fridge into three cookable dinner suggestions for busy home cooks.
project.primary_user: A busy home cook who stares at a full fridge with no dinner idea.
project.core_problem: Recipe sites answer 'what can I cook in general', not 'what can I cook right now with what I have'; the gap between inventory and inspiration stays manual.
project.core_journey:
1. The cook photographs the open fridge.
2. The app extracts an ingredient list and shows it for one-tap correction.
3. The app retrieves three matching recipes and adapts each to the confirmed inventory.
4. The cook picks one and gets a step-by-step cooking view.
project.success_measure: A first-time user goes from photo to a chosen, cookable recipe in under two minutes without editing more than two ingredients.
project.success_metrics:
- photo-to-recipe time -- p95 under 2 minutes
- ingredient extraction accuracy -- fewer than 2 manual corrections per photo
- suggestion acceptance -- at least 1 of 3 suggestions chosen in 60% of sessions
project.riskiest_assumption: Vision-model ingredient extraction is accurate enough on real, messy fridge photos to need at most two corrections.
project.req_ac_list:
- [ ] **REQ-AC1:** Uploading a clear fridge photo yields an editable ingredient list within 15 seconds.
- [ ] **REQ-AC2:** Confirming the list yields exactly three recipe suggestions, each cookable with the confirmed ingredients plus pantry staples.
- [ ] **REQ-AC3:** Choosing a suggestion opens a step-by-step cooking view with servings scaled to the household size.
- [ ] **REQ-AC4:** A photo with no recognizable food produces a helpful empty state, not a crash or a hallucinated list.
project.non_goals:
- No meal planning across multiple days; one dinner at a time.
- No grocery ordering or shopping-list integration in the first iteration.
- No user accounts beyond a device-local profile.
project.other_users:
- Meal-prep enthusiasts planning several dinners at once (deferred).
- Dietitians reviewing a client's typical inventory (deferred).
project.constraint_time: 8-week side project; first demo at week 4.
project.constraint_cost: LLM/API budget capped at USD 30/month during development; each photo-to-recipes flow must cost under USD 0.05.
project.constraint_data: Recipes from a licensed open dataset (Recipe1M subset); user photos never leave the pipeline and are deleted after extraction.
project.deployment_target: Public web app (mobile-first), hosted on a single VPS first.
project.scale_expectations: Tens of users, low hundreds of photos per week in the first iteration; p95 photo-to-suggestions under 15 seconds.
project.integrations:
- OpenAI vision + embeddings API (extraction, retrieval).
- Chroma vector store (local, embedded).
project.in_scope_list:
- Fridge-photo upload and ingredient extraction with one-tap correction.
- Three recipe suggestions adapted to the confirmed inventory.
- Step-by-step cooking view with serving scaling.
- Helpful empty/error states for unusable photos.
project.pain_point: stares at a full fridge with no dinner idea
project.current_alternative: scrolling recipe sites and guessing what matches the fridge
project.key_benefit: turns what you have into what you can cook tonight
project.key_differentiator: start from a photo of the actual fridge, not from a search box
